# UC-18.2.9 · VMware NSX Transport Node Overlay Path Health (End-to-End GENEVE Path Quality, Per-TEP-Pair Packet Loss / Jitter / RTT, MTU Validation, Probe-based Active Measurement, Beyond Binary UP/DOWN UC-18.2.5)

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Performance, Fault &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We measure how cleanly the invisible overlay highways carry packets between server hosts—not merely whether tunnels answer yes or no—so shaky paths get repaired before everyday applications feel the drag.*

---

## Description

BFD tells you whether an overlay tunnel endpoint answers yes-or-no before timers expire; overlay **path quality** measures gradients—fractional loss, milliseconds of delay, jitter swings, ECMP imbalance—long before tunnels declare DOWN. A fabric can keep tunnels UP while still delivering miserable east-west behavior: half-percent packet loss converts cleanly into TCP retransmit storms; fifty milliseconds RTT on an intra-rack pair reads healthy on dashboards yet violates latency-sensitive workloads; path-MTU shrinkage triggers fragmentation or black-hole behaviors where DF probes succeed sporadically. GENEVE outer encapsulation consumes roughly fifty bytes of overhead on the wire—UDP plus IP plus Ethernet framing plus GENEVE header plus typical option blocks—so expecting **1700** bytes effective envelopes versus **8950** jumbo envelopes matters when operators assume "large frames everywhere". This UC deliberately complements UC-18.2.5's binary BFD posture by spotlighting sustained degradation signals sourced from Path Health probes, REST snapshots, syslog alarms (`OVERLAY_PATH_DEGRADED`, `MTU_MISMATCH`, `TUNNEL_PERFORMANCE_LOW`), and scripted dataplane counters—not duplicate tunnel UP/DOWN churn.

## Value

Unified **`vmware:nsxt:tn_diag`** telemetry spanning **`src_tep_ip`** × **`dst_tep_ip`** pairs lets operators enforce overlay quality service levels—typically below **`0.1`** percent **`pkt_loss_pct`**, single-digit **`rtt_ms`** inside one metro, sub-millisecond jitter budgets where workloads demand it—without waiting for Tier-1 incidents after latency piles up silently. Splunk charts spotlight predictive degradation—rising **`pkt_loss_pct`** ahead of BFD flip events; **`mtu_path`** readings underneath advertised **`mtu_used`** ceilings that imply fragmentation along intermediate hops; and **`probe_outcome`** regressions sourced from **`Path Health`** polls whose cadence (**60** seconds stock; **10** seconds aggressive clusters) you annotate beside dashboards so reviewers judge streaks versus sampling glitch. Aggregation ladders—worst **`TEP-pair`** per rack slice, worst transport-node umbrella across uplinks, worst campus facet when **`site_code`** lookups exist—accelerate escalation toward switching crews reconciling **`tor_maintenance_window`** tags, capacity planners forecasting **`TEP`** pool exhaustion inside **`/16`** or **`/20`** **`VTEP`** allocations, and architects contrasting intra-rack milliseconds versus stretched-cluster geography expectations.

## Implementation

Install **Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt** (Splunkbase **4856**) for **`vmware:nsxt:syslog`** parsing; add scripted HTTPS collectors landing **`vmware:nsxt:tn_diag`** samples keyed by **`src_tn_id`**, **`dst_tn_id`**, **`src_tep_ip`**, **`dst_tep_ip`**, **`pkt_loss_pct`**, **`rtt_ms`**, **`jitter_ms`**, **`mtu_path`**, **`probe_outcome`**; optionally harvest **`vmware:nsxt:transport_node`** REST mirrors plus **`esxi:nic:stats`** where **`nsxdp-cli`** exporters exist; schedule **`macros/nsxt_overlay_path_quality`** alerts with CMDB-backed suppression.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Install **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** ([Splunkbase 4856](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) wherever **`vmware:nsxt:syslog`** parsing runs for VMware NSX 4.x or NSX-T 3.2.x aligned with Broadcom lifecycle guidance.
- Stand up HTTPS collectors for tunnel inventory and tunnel stats REST routes (`GET /api/v1/transport-nodes/{tn-id}/tunnels`, `GET /api/v1/transport-nodes/{tn-id}/tunnels/{tunnel-id}/stats`) without granting **`DELETE`** verbs—authenticate via **`POST /api/session/create`** over **`TCP 443`**, pin cipher suites per VMware hardening guides, and monitor **`splunkd.log`** whenever **`429`** or **`502`** implies **`NSX Manager`** API throttling ahead of Splunk ingest gaps.
- Normalize **`vmware:nsxt:tn_diag`** JSON so fields `src_*`, `dst_*`, `pkt_loss_pct`, `rtt_ms`, `jitter_ms`, `mtu_*`, `probe_outcome`, and `_time` align with NSX Manager Path Health screens (System ▸ Fabric ▸ Hosts ▸ `<host>` ▸ Tunnels ▸ Path Health).
- Budget indexer licensing when thousands of **`TEP-pair`** tuples arrive each sweep—60 second default probes versus 10 second aggressive cadences materially shift EPS envelopes.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Deploy **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** syslog listeners toward **`vmware:nsxt:syslog`** streams retaining **`OVERLAY_PATH_DEGRADED`**, **`MTU_MISMATCH`**, and **`TUNNEL_PERFORMANCE_LOW`** signatures alongside WARN/ERROR severities emitted by VMware NSX alarm pipelines.
(2) Author modular Python inputs on Heavy Forwarders polling **`GET /api/v1/transport-nodes/{id}/tunnels`** plus **`GET /api/v1/transport-nodes/{id}/tunnels/{tunnel-id}/stats`**, emitting newline JSON into **`vmware:nsxt:tn_diag`** keyed by **`tunnel-id`** GUID mirrors beside **`bfd_session_status`** pointers inside REST payloads for UC-18.2.5 correlation hooks.
(3) Maintain **`vmware:nsxt:transport_node`** Policy snapshots (**`GET /api/v1/transport-nodes`**) so **`realization`** breadcrumbs remain visible whenever overlay segments stall versus tunnel telemetry gaps alone—distinct from optional **`vmware:nsxt:realization`** feeds reserved for logical-object audits outside pure **`pkt_loss_pct`** scopes.
(4) Schedule Universal Forwarder scripts invoking **`nsxdp-cli netcpa stats get`**, **`nsxdp-cli geneve get`**, **`nsxdp-cli vlanstats overlay-pnic-rx-drop`**, and **`nsxdp-cli vswitch instance list`** so GENEVE encaps counters enrich **`esxi:nic:stats`** pipelines already harvesting **`vmnic`** telemetry emphasized inside UC-18.2.17 sibling scopes without collapsing scopes here.
(5) During Sev1 bridges capture **`nsxcli -c "get logical-overlay-tunnel <tunnel-id> stats"`** CLI parity manually or via gated scripted **`inputs`** when governance permits ephemeral dataplane transcripts beside Splunk **`_time`** correlation identifiers.

### Step 2 — SPL search and alert configuration
Paste the **`spl`** field into **`macros/nsxt_overlay_path_quality`** once **`index`** aliases stabilize across **`vmware`**, **`vmware_nsx`**, and **`network_ops`** tiers.

```spl
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx OR index=network_ops)
(sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:tn_diag" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:transport_node" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:syslog")
earliest=-4h@m
| eval src_key=trim(coalesce(src_tn_id,src_transport_node_id,src_node_uuid,""))
| eval dst_key=trim(coalesce(dst_tn_id,dst_transport_node_id,dst_node_uuid,""))
| eval tep_src=trim(coalesce(src_tep_ip,src_te_ip,vtep_src_addr,""))
| eval tep_dst=trim(coalesce(dst_tep_ip,dst_te_ip,vtep_dst_addr,""))
| eval loss_pct=tonumber(coalesce(pkt_loss_pct,packet_loss_percent,overlay_loss_pct,"0"))
| eval rtt_ms_num=tonumber(coalesce(rtt_ms,rtt_avg_ms,overlay_rtt_ms,"0"))
| eval jitter_ms_num=tonumber(coalesce(jitter_ms,jitter_avg_ms,overlay_jitter_ms,"0"))
| eval mtu_path_num=tonumber(coalesce(mtu_path,discovered_path_mtu,pmtud_mtu,"0"))
| eval mtu_used_num=tonumber(coalesce(mtu_used,configured_mtu,advertised_mtu,"0"))
| eval mtu_issue_txt=upper(trim(coalesce(mtu_issue,mtu_problem_flag,"")))
| eval probe_txt=upper(trim(coalesce(probe_outcome,probe_result,path_health_outcome,"")))
| eval syslog_alarm=case(
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)OVERLAY_PATH_DEGRADED"),"OVERLAY_PATH_DEGRADED",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)MTU_MISMATCH"),"MTU_MISMATCH",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)TUNNEL_PERFORMANCE_LOW"),"TUNNEL_PERFORMANCE_LOW",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(geneve.?path|overlay.?quality|path.?health)"),"OVERLAY_QUALITY_SYSLOG",
    null())
| eval quality_fault=case(
    sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:syslog" AND isnotnull(syslog_alarm), syslog_alarm,
    sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:tn_diag" AND loss_pct>=0.1,"pkt_loss_pct_high",
    sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:tn_diag" AND jitter_ms_num>=1,"jitter_ms_high",
    sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:tn_diag" AND mtu_issue_txt IN ("YES","TRUE","Y","1"),"mtu_issue_true",
    sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:tn_diag" AND mtu_path_num>0 AND mtu_used_num>0 AND mtu_path_num<(mtu_used_num-400),"mtu_path_below_expectation",
    sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:tn_diag" AND probe_txt IN ("FAIL","FAILED","DEGRADED","BAD"),"probe_outcome_bad",
    sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:tn_diag" AND rtt_ms_num>=50 AND match(tep_src,".+"),"rtt_ms_elevated_watch",
    sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:transport_node" AND match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(overlay.?tunnel|path.?health|tep.?pair|tunnel.?stats)"),"transport_node_overlay_context",
    null())
| where isnotnull(quality_fault)
| bin _time span=5m
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen,
    max(loss_pct) as worst_pkt_loss_pct,
    max(rtt_ms_num) as worst_rtt_ms,
    max(jitter_ms_num) as worst_jitter_ms,
    latest(mtu_path_num) as mtu_path_latest,
    latest(mtu_used_num) as mtu_used_latest,
    values(tep_src) as src_tep_ip_seen,
    values(tep_dst) as dst_tep_ip_seen,
    latest(syslog_alarm) as syslog_alarm_latest,
    dc(tep_src) as tep_src_cardinality,
    count as evt_cnt
  by _time src_key dst_key quality_fault
| eval tep_pair_hint=mvjoin(sort(mvdedup(mvappend(src_tep_ip_seen,dst_tep_ip_seen))),",")
| sort -last_seen
| head 500
| table last_seen src_key dst_key quality_fault worst_pkt_loss_pct worst_rtt_ms worst_jitter_ms mtu_path_latest mtu_used_latest src_tep_ip_seen dst_tep_ip_seen syslog_alarm_latest tep_src_cardinality tep_pair_hint evt_cnt
```

Tune pkt_loss_pct_high thresholds starting at ≥0.1 percent sustained bursts—tighten toward ≥0.05 once baselines mature for latency-sensitive tiers. mtu_path_below_expectation compares **`mtu_path_num`** against **`mtu_used_num`** using a 400 byte gap—relax when fabrics advertise **`8950`** effective envelopes minus roughly fifty-byte GENEVE overhead (9000 minus 50 aligns with **`8950`** guidance cited beside VMware MTU references linked below).

Throttle posture — suppress **`src_key`** · **`dst_key`** tuple repeats for four hours unless **`OVERLAY_PATH_DEGRADED`** syslog persists concurrently across successive stats buckets implying structural—not transient—fault signatures independent from **`probe_interval_sampling_artefact`** chatter summarized inside knownFalsePositives.

### Step 3 — Validate against VMware consoles
(1) Open NSX Manager Path Health blades (System ▸ Fabric ▸ Hosts ▸ `<host>` ▸ Tunnels ▸ Path Health) comparing **`pkt_loss_pct`**, **`rtt_ms`**, and **`jitter_ms`** curves versus Splunk aggregates ± one probe interval skew allowances annotated beside dashboards.
(2) SSH ESXi hosts and execute **`nsxcli -c "get logical-overlay-tunnel <tunnel-id> stats"`** referencing **`tunnel-id`** GUID mirrors lifted from **`GET /api/v1/transport-nodes/{id}/tunnels`** responses captured inside **`vmware:nsxt:transport_node`** extracts—avoid trusting Splunk-only narratives absent CLI corroboration during Sev1 bridges.
(3) Correlate degraded **`quality_fault`** buckets with **`OVERLAY_PATH_DEGRADED`** narratives arriving inside **`vmware:nsxt:syslog`** streams rather than orphaned syslog floods lacking **`tn_diag`** counterparts signaling ingestion imbalance toward Heavy Forwarders hosting modular collectors.
(4) Validate **`mtu_path`** versus **`mtu_used`** deltas against DF ping sweeps documented inside networking runbooks—GENEVE stacks roughly fifty-byte outer framing (UDP, IP, Ethernet, GENEVE, optional TLV headers) aligning with **`1700`** minimum envelopes versus **`8950`** jumbo envelopes minus encaps overhead cited throughout VMware docs referenced below.
(5) Reach for **`vmware:nsxt:realization`** feeds only when suspicion focuses on stale controllers—not dataplane degradation—because realization narratives intentionally diverge from Path Health KPI semantics emphasized herein.

### Step 4 — Operationalize dashboards
- Plot overlay loss matrices using **`src_tep_ip`** and **`dst_tep_ip`** axes colored by **`worst_pkt_loss_pct`** so asymmetric hotspots remain visible instead of collapsing measurements behind fleet averages that disguise ECMP imbalance across racks.
- Layer **`worst_rtt_ms`** lines with **`worst_jitter_ms`** annotations whenever probe schedules mix 60 second defaults beside 10 second VIP schedules—label panels so reviewers separate sampling artefacts from WAN-side variance on stretched fabrics.
- Contrast **`mtu_path_latest`** against **`mtu_used_latest`** in an MTU anomaly lane whenever fragmentation signatures appear despite BFD UP posture already tracked separately inside UC-18.2.5 dashboards shown beside—not blended with—Path Health KPI tiles from this UC.
- Rank Top-N paths by **`worst_pkt_loss_pct`** (cap near 500 rows) and feed paging macros that honor **`tor_maintenance_window`** suppressions pulled nightly from CMDB bridges tied to switching crews—not VMware SMEs alone—when alarms align with leaf maintenance narratives logged inside **`CHG`** repositories.
- Plot **`tep_pool_used_percent`** beside—not inside—core SPL macros because pool exhaustion behaves differently from sustained **`pkt_loss_pct`** pathology.

### Step 5 — Tuning & extensions
- Correlate **`quality_fault`** streaks with UC-18.2.5 **`tunnel_state_latest`** and **`bfd_latest`** rows whenever **`pkt_loss_pct`** climbs yet BFD stays UP—those splits isolate overlay-quality regressions requiring probe—not failover—responses coordinated across networking plus virtualization crews referencing UC-18.2.5 narratives centered on binary posture rather than gradients tracked here.
- Touch UC-18.2.17 **`vmnic`** counters only after overlay hypotheses weaken—pure **`pkt_loss_pct`** anomalies referencing **`GENEVE`** overlays stay separate from **`CRC`**/**`FCS`** physics covered in the pNIC UC.
- Keep UC-18.2.4 Tier-0/Tier-1 north-south dashboards visually separate; Edge **`DPDK`** saturation differs materially from east-west TEP-pair overlays emphasized in this UC.
- Tie suppressions to **`tor_maintenance_window`** CMDB tags plus **`CHG`** CSV feeds before paging **`OVERLAY_PATH_DEGRADED`** bursts that map to switching maintenance instead of VMware-only runbooks—aligning with **`lag_rebalance_window`** commentary inside **`knownFalsePositives`** without collapsing unrelated sibling scopes.
- Optional Arista EOS/Nexus interface counters plus **`LLDP`** neighbors gathered via **`esxcli`** remain enrichment-only whenever **`mtu_path_below_expectation`** persists beyond **`mtu_renegotiation_convergence`** windows—still bridging **`overlay`** versus **`underlay`** accountability without pretending UC-18.2.14 Intelligence rankings substitute Path Health KPI semantics summarized herein.

Troubleshooting quick hits
- Empty **`tn_diag`** feeds usually mean stalled modular collectors missing **`tunnel-id`** expansions—inspect Heavy Forwarder **`splunkd.log`** Python traces referencing **`429`**, **`401`**, or **`502`** responses from **`NSX Manager`** HTTPS fronts before rewriting parsers blindly.
- **`transport_node_overlay_context`** storms imply regex breadth—tighten **`_raw`** filters toward literal path-health tokens whenever syslog cardinality drowns actionable **`pkt_loss_pct`** streaks.
- High evt_cnt with probe_cpu_saturation suspicions requires ESXi **`CPU`** readiness checks before blaming WAN optics when probes originate CPU-starved hosts—aligns with virtualization runbooks summarized inside knownFalsePositives.
- **`lag_rebalance_window`** masks need dual evidence: pair **`worst_pkt_loss_pct`** jumps with absent syslog_alarm_latest rows before escalating sole dataplane jitter.
- Pool **`percent_used`** charts belong next to—not inside—the core SPL because **`tep_pool_exhaustion_onboarding`** bursts differ from sustained **`pkt_loss_pct`** escalation deserving distinct capacity workflows referencing **`VTEP`** stewardship dashboards.

## SPL

```spl
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx OR index=network_ops)
(sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:tn_diag" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:transport_node" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:syslog")
earliest=-4h@m
| eval src_key=trim(coalesce(src_tn_id,src_transport_node_id,src_node_uuid,""))
| eval dst_key=trim(coalesce(dst_tn_id,dst_transport_node_id,dst_node_uuid,""))
| eval tep_src=trim(coalesce(src_tep_ip,src_te_ip,vtep_src_addr,""))
| eval tep_dst=trim(coalesce(dst_tep_ip,dst_te_ip,vtep_dst_addr,""))
| eval loss_pct=tonumber(coalesce(pkt_loss_pct,packet_loss_percent,overlay_loss_pct,"0"))
| eval rtt_ms_num=tonumber(coalesce(rtt_ms,rtt_avg_ms,overlay_rtt_ms,"0"))
| eval jitter_ms_num=tonumber(coalesce(jitter_ms,jitter_avg_ms,overlay_jitter_ms,"0"))
| eval mtu_path_num=tonumber(coalesce(mtu_path,discovered_path_mtu,pmtud_mtu,"0"))
| eval mtu_used_num=tonumber(coalesce(mtu_used,configured_mtu,advertised_mtu,"0"))
| eval mtu_issue_txt=upper(trim(coalesce(mtu_issue,mtu_problem_flag,"")))
| eval probe_txt=upper(trim(coalesce(probe_outcome,probe_result,path_health_outcome,"")))
| eval syslog_alarm=case(
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)OVERLAY_PATH_DEGRADED"),"OVERLAY_PATH_DEGRADED",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)MTU_MISMATCH"),"MTU_MISMATCH",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)TUNNEL_PERFORMANCE_LOW"),"TUNNEL_PERFORMANCE_LOW",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(geneve.?path|overlay.?quality|path.?health)"),"OVERLAY_QUALITY_SYSLOG",
    null())
| eval quality_fault=case(
    sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:syslog" AND isnotnull(syslog_alarm), syslog_alarm,
    sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:tn_diag" AND loss_pct>=0.1,"pkt_loss_pct_high",
    sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:tn_diag" AND jitter_ms_num>=1,"jitter_ms_high",
    sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:tn_diag" AND mtu_issue_txt IN ("YES","TRUE","Y","1"),"mtu_issue_true",
    sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:tn_diag" AND mtu_path_num>0 AND mtu_used_num>0 AND mtu_path_num<(mtu_used_num-400),"mtu_path_below_expectation",
    sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:tn_diag" AND probe_txt IN ("FAIL","FAILED","DEGRADED","BAD"),"probe_outcome_bad",
    sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:tn_diag" AND rtt_ms_num>=50 AND match(tep_src,".+"),"rtt_ms_elevated_watch",
    sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:transport_node" AND match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(overlay.?tunnel|path.?health|tep.?pair|tunnel.?stats)"),"transport_node_overlay_context",
    null())
| where isnotnull(quality_fault)
| bin _time span=5m
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen,
    max(loss_pct) as worst_pkt_loss_pct,
    max(rtt_ms_num) as worst_rtt_ms,
    max(jitter_ms_num) as worst_jitter_ms,
    latest(mtu_path_num) as mtu_path_latest,
    latest(mtu_used_num) as mtu_used_latest,
    values(tep_src) as src_tep_ip_seen,
    values(tep_dst) as dst_tep_ip_seen,
    latest(syslog_alarm) as syslog_alarm_latest,
    dc(tep_src) as tep_src_cardinality,
    count as evt_cnt
  by _time src_key dst_key quality_fault
| eval tep_pair_hint=mvjoin(mvsort(mvdedup(mvappend(src_tep_ip_seen,dst_tep_ip_seen))),",")
| sort -last_seen
| head 500
| table last_seen src_key dst_key quality_fault worst_pkt_loss_pct worst_rtt_ms worst_jitter_ms mtu_path_latest mtu_used_latest src_tep_ip_seen dst_tep_ip_seen syslog_alarm_latest tep_src_cardinality tep_pair_hint evt_cnt
```

## Visualization

(1) Matrix heat-map: **`src_tep_ip`** × **`dst_tep_ip`** coloring **`worst_pkt_loss_pct`** buckets. (2) Time-series: **`worst_rtt_ms`** and **`worst_jitter_ms`** overlays split by **`quality_fault`**. (3) Choropleth-style rack facets when **`rack_id`** lookups exist—otherwise facet **`site_code`**. (4) Table of worst **`src_key`** / **`dst_key`** pairs with **`mtu_path_latest`** versus **`mtu_used_latest`**. (5) Single-value tiles tracking **`OVERLAY_PATH_DEGRADED`** syslog bursts versus **`pkt_loss_pct_high`** diag samples.

## Known False Positives

**`tor_maintenance_window`** — Planned leaf-switch upgrades deliberately perturb east-west forwarding across specific racks; Path Health probes traverse the impacted underlay hops and may show temporary loss or RTT inflation that mirrors outages while dataplane forwarding recovers post-cut. Anchor suppressions to **`CHG`** identifiers plus CMDB **`tor_maintenance_window=true`** tags instead of paging overlay teams alone when underlay orchestration owns the bridge. 

**`mtu_renegotiation_convergence`** — Upstream switches sometimes re-stage path-MTU during VXLAN/vPC or storm-control refits; **`mtu_path`** can read hundreds of bytes beneath **`mtu_used`** until DF probes finish their five-to-ten minute sweep. Treat these as transitional noise when NSX Manager UI already shows **`mtu_issue`** clearing across successive polls rather than steady fragmentation signatures. 

**`probe_cpu_saturation`** — Path Health originates from hypervisor dataplane contexts; when the originating TN spikes ESXi **`CPU`** readiness queues, probe scheduling skew inflates **`rtt_ms`** despite calm intermediate hops—confirm host **`CPU`** contention (`res cpu`) before accusing WAN optics. 

**`lag_rebalance_window`** — Active-active NIC teams rebalance hashing briefly after uplink flap remediation or firmware pushes; asymmetric hashing yields uneven **`pkt_loss_pct`** samples until hashing tables steady—suppress against **`CHG`** stamps referencing **`LAG`**/`team` bridges and corroborate with switch **`LAG`** counters rather than Splunk-only escalation. 

**`multi_dc_stretched_latency_variance`** — Geographically stretched clusters intentionally absorb tens of milliseconds RTT during peak replication windows; **`rtt_ms`** variance tracks geography more than pathology—tie thresholds to **`metro_pair`** tags instead of intra-rack goals. 

**`tep_pool_exhaustion_onboarding`** — Massive TN onboarding spikes allocate **`src_tep_ip`** leases quickly yet probe matrices populate slowly; dashboards briefly show **`transport_node_overlay_context`** gaps resembling silent degradation until **`probe_outcome`** rows hydrate—monitor pool **`percent_used`** separately before paging. 

**`probe_interval_sampling_artefact`** — Operators sometimes tighten Path Health schedules (**10** seconds) on VIP clusters while defaults remain **60** seconds elsewhere; **`pkt_loss_pct`** spikes reflect Nyquist-style sampling mismatches rather than asymmetric fabrics—normalize comparisons using **`probe_interval_sec`** tags when dual cadences coexist. 

**`drs_host_evacuation_oscillation`** — VMware **`DRS`** drains relocate VMs aggressively during contention remediation; transporting workloads reshuffles **`src_tep_ip`** anchors rapidly so **`worst_pkt_loss_pct`** oscillates minute-to-minute despite benign median paths—defer paging until evacuation banners clear.

## References

- [VMware NSX Documentation — Fabric > Transport Nodes > Overlay Tunnel Path Health](https://techdocs.broadcom.com/us/en/vmware-cis/nsx/vmware-nsx/4-2/administration-guide.html)
- [Splunk Add-on for VMware NSX-T (Splunkbase 4856)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)
- [VMware NSX MTU Guidance (GENEVE overlays)](https://techdocs.broadcom.com/us/en/vmware-cis/nsx/vmware-nsx/4-0/mtu-guidance.html)
- [Splunk Lantern — Operational telemetry guidance](https://lantern.splunk.com/)
